mechanism_narrative: High and rising FEDFUNDS indicates a tight monetary policy stance, which increases the cost of capital and reduces corporate profitability expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS is rising, or the walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a certain threshold indicates a strong labor market, supporting robust consumer spending and corporate earnings, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where UNRATE is below 5.0%, or the average return in such periods is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales (RSAFS) combined with robust housing starts (HOUST) indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand, boosting corporate revenues and leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) during a period of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) signals stagflationary pressures, which erodes corporate margins and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during high inflation regimes, or the interaction effect is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the FEDFUNDS rate exceeds a certain level, it signals an overly restrictive monetary policy that chokes off economic growth, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 50% of periods where FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or the average return in such periods is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in GDP (GDPC1) indicates a healthy expanding economy, which supports corporate earnings and investor confidence, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 55% of periods where GDPC1 is growing, or the walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong employment growth (PAYEMS) combined with robust retail sales (RSAFS) indicates a powerful consumer-driven economy, which directly translates to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction term between PAYEMS and RSAFS growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A high inflation rate (CPIAUCSL) combined with a high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates an aggressive monetary policy response to overheating, which typically precedes an economic slowdown and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the combined effect of high inflation and high rates does not lead to significantly negative returns (t-test p > 0.05).
